text.title: PostToolUse hook injects validator stderr as feedback
text.summary: Wire linters and typecheckers into PostToolUse hooks so validator failures appear to the agent as tool errors and trigger automatic corrections on the next turn.

section.title: Validator-as-feedback rule
section.p: The agent already knows how to react to a tool error: retry with a fix. PostToolUse hooks let you piggyback on that mechanic — every Write or Edit triggers a validator, and a non-zero exit code is fed back as the next turn's tool-result error message.

rule.statement: For every code-editing agent, configure a PostToolUse hook with matcher "Write|Edit" that runs the project's mandatory validator (ruff, eslint, tsc, pytest -x, schema validate) on the affected file. Failures must exit non-zero so stderr is surfaced to the agent.
rule.rationale: Hook stderr is wired by Claude Code into the next tool-result message. The model is RLHF-trained to react to tool errors by editing the previous output; the hook converts "ask the agent to lint" into "the agent sees lint failure and fixes it" with zero prompt overhead.

example.note: Validator scoped to the mutated file; non-zero exit on lint failure flows back as tool error; agent self-corrects.

example.code: You are a Python agent. After every edit, run `ruff check` on the file you changed and fix any errors before continuing.
example.note: Prompt-based instruction relies on the model remembering. Long contexts skip the check exactly when it matters.

antipattern.description: PostToolUse hook that runs the full project lint (ruff check .) instead of the changed file.
antipattern.reason: Latency scales with project size, hook stalls every Edit, and unrelated pre-existing failures pollute the agent's view of its own change.

antipattern.description: Slow validators (mypy strict on the whole repo, integration tests) wired as PostToolUse.
antipattern.reason: Each Edit blocks for seconds-to-minutes — the agent thrashes and contexts blow up. Move slow checks to PreCommit or background tasks.

section.reference: Reference settings.json wiring ruff (Python) and eslint+tsc (TypeScript) on Write|Edit.
Claude Code Hooks guide — matcher syntax and exit-code semantics.
Agent SDK hooks reference — programmatic hook registration.

section.title: What the agent sees on failure
section.p: When the hook exits non-zero, Claude Code injects the captured stderr as a synthetic tool error attached to the just-completed Write/Edit. The agent's next turn receives this error in its message stream and proposes a corrective edit without a separate prompt.

list.item: Hook exit 0 — silent, agent continues as if nothing happened.
Hook exit non-zero — stderr surfaces as tool-result error, agent retries with fix.
Hook stdout is shown to the agent only via a structured tool result; prefer stderr for failure detail.
Set a per-hook timeout (default 60s) — long-running validators stall the loop.
